top_issues.title: Low Speed Pre-Ignition (LSPI) detonation on 1.0T petrol engines
top_issues.description: Some 1.0-litre turbocharged petrol engines have suffered from LSPI, a form of engine knock that can cause severe internal damage. Vauxhall has replaced affected engines, but owners report that replacement units have also failed, suggesting possible block damage that may not be fully repaired during exchange.
typical_symptoms: Knocking or pinging noise from engine under light acceleration
Loss of power or engine failure

top_issues.title: Timing chain failure on 1.3 CDTI diesel engines
top_issues.description: At least one report of a timing chain failing at 60,000 miles on a 1.3-litre diesel engine. Timing chain failure is a serious issue that can cause catastrophic engine damage if not caught early. Maintenance history is important, as poor servicing can accelerate wear.
typical_symptoms: Rattling noise from front of engine, especially on cold start

top_issues.title: Coil pack failures on petrol engines
top_issues.description: Multiple reports of coil packs failing prematurely, with some owners experiencing repeat failures within a few years. Coil pack failure causes misfires and poor running, and replacement is straightforward but can happen more than once on the same vehicle.
typical_symptoms: Rough idle or hesitation under acceleration

top_issues.title: Automatic gearbox entering limp mode intermittently
top_issues.description: Some 1.4-litre torque converter automatic gearboxes have entered limp mode without a stored fault code, making diagnosis difficult. The fault clears when the engine is restarted, but can recur unpredictably. Dealers have struggled to identify the root cause.
typical_symptoms: Yellow spanner warning light on dashboard
Severe loss of power or car will barely move

top_issues.title: Vibration from doors, dashboard and hatchback on early models
top_issues.description: At least one pre-registered 2017 model suffered persistent vibrations from the doors, dashboard and hatchback that could not be resolved despite multiple dealer repairs including door replacement and dashboard removal. Vauxhall declined to replace the vehicle and stated the vibration was a characteristic of the model.
typical_symptoms: Noticeable vibration felt through doors when driving

overall_reliability_note: The 2014–2019 Corsa is generally a competent small hatchback with an improved interior and good refinement, but buyers should be aware of potential engine issues on early 1.0T petrol models, timing chain concerns on diesel variants, and occasional gearbox faults on automatic versions.
